refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, optimization logic, and operator awareness. This supports both direct operation and teleoperation modes.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_consumed / total_tonnage_moved over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 40-hour work week). Verify that measured value ≤ E_target. Collect telemetry from Vehicle power systems and cross-reference with load/distance data. Conduct controlled trials under representative worksite conditions with both on-site and tele-operated modes.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log tonnage moved (mass × distance or equivalent work metric).
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall implement control optimization logic to minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall support energy efficiency reporting and analytics for Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Passive energy monitoring only (measure and log, no active optimization)
DP-B: Active energy optimization with real-time feedback loop (measure → analyze → optimize control → feedback)
DP-C: Hybrid approach with operator-guided optimization (measure → analyze → recommend → operator adjusts)
DP-D: Distributed optimization across Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems with coordinated control
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-B: Active energy optimization with real-time feedback loop. The NGHE solution shall implement an integrated energy management system that continuously measures energy consumption and work output, applies optimization algorithms to control parameters (e.g., engine throttle, transmission efficiency, auxiliary load shedding), and provides real-time feedback to operators and tele-operators to reinforce efficient behavior.

potential_sources: Energy optimization logic is coupled to Vehicle propulsion and control systems (expected and necessary).
Real-time feedback is coupled to Teleoperation network link (acceptable; latency requirements must be defined).
Safety constraints (e.g., minimum power for emergency maneuvers) must be decoupled from efficiency optimization via explicit safety gates.
coupling_check.notes: DP-B exhibits functional coupling (energy optimization depends on Vehicle control) but this is intentional and manageable. No problematic information coupling detected. Safety decoupling is critical and must be enforced in DP refinement.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Energy consumption can be measured with sufficient accuracy and granularity (per subsystem or aggregate). Risk: Sensor noise or calibration drift could invalidate efficiency metrics.

Assumption: Optimization algorithms can be tuned to respect safety constraints without degrading efficiency gains. Risk: Conflicting objectives (efficiency vs. safety margin) may require trade-off analysis.
Assumption: Tele-operators have sufficient network latency and bandwidth to receive real-time feedback and adjust controls. Risk: Network delays could degrade feedback effectiveness.
Assumption: Operators will respond to efficiency feedback and modify behavior. Risk: Behavioral adoption is uncertain; training and incentive structures are external to the system.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Refine DP-B to explicitly separate the safety-critical control layer (which must always prioritize safety) from the efficiency optimization layer (which operates within safety constraints). Define a safety gate that prevents optimization from reducing power reserves below a threshold required for emergency response. Specify feedback latency requirements (e.g., <500 ms for tele-operated mode) to ensure real-time effectiveness.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary that integrates Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services subsystems. For CR-NGHE-003, the NGHE Solution module owns the energy management system logic, including measurement aggregation, optimization algorithms, feedback generation, and reporting. The module coordinates energy data flows across Vehicle (propulsion/auxiliary power) and Teleoperation (feedback delivery) and enforces safety gates via Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution outputs real-time energy efficiency metrics (energy_per_ton, instantaneous power, cumulative energy, work output) to operators and tele-operators via Telemetry interface. Frequency: ≥1 Hz for real-time feedback; aggregated summaries at lower frequency for reporting.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution receives work tasking (load, destination, priority) from Site Manager or autonomous planning layer via Tasking interface. Tasking informs energy optimization context (e.g., energy budget for a task, preferred efficiency vs. speed trade-off).

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution exchanges safety constraints and alerts with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface. Inbound: safety limits (min power reserve, no-optimization zones). Outbound: energy-related safety events (e.g., power depletion risk, optimization failure).

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: NGHE Solution uses Network Link to communicate with Teleoperation subsystem for real-time feedback delivery and to receive tele-operator control inputs that may affect energy optimization.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner of energy management logic. Energy optimization is a system-level concern that coordinates Vehicle and Teleoperation subsystems. Minimal diffusion: only measurement data flows to Vehicle (for sensor integration) and feedback flows to Teleoperation (for display). High containment is appropriate.

Vehicle could own energy measurement and local optimization (e.g., engine efficiency tuning, transmission control). However, system-level optimization (balancing energy vs. productivity, cross-subsystem coordination) requires NGHE Solution ownership. If energy logic is split between Vehicle and NGHE Solution, diffusion risk is high (duplicate algorithms, inconsistent optimization goals).

Teleoperation is responsible for feedback delivery and tele-operator interface, not energy optimization logic. Assigning energy management to Teleoperation would create high diffusion (logic scattered across on-site and remote systems) and poor cohesion. Not recommended.

Safety Services owns safety constraints and gates, not energy optimization. However, Safety Services must provide safety limits to NGHE Solution (e.g., min power reserve). Assigning energy optimization to Safety Services would conflate safety and efficiency concerns. Not recommended.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the optimal owner of energy management logic because: (1) energy efficiency is a system-level KPI that requires coordination across Vehicle (power generation/consumption) and Teleoperation (feedback delivery); (2) centralizing optimization logic in NGHE Solution avoids diffusion and ensures consistent algorithms; (3) NGHE Solution can enforce safety gates via SafetyAlert interface without duplicating safety logic; (4) NGHE Solution can aggregate telemetry from Vehicle and deliver coordinated feedback via Telemetry interface.
containment_risks: Risk: If Vehicle subsystem also implements local energy optimization (e.g., engine efficiency tuning), there is potential for conflicting optimization goals or duplicate algorithms. Mitigation: Define clear ownership boundaries—Vehicle owns low-level power control; NGHE Solution owns system-level optimization logic.

Risk: Real-time feedback delivery via Teleoperation may introduce latency that degrades optimization effectiveness. Mitigation: Specify latency requirements (<500 ms) and implement local feedback loops in Vehicle for immediate response.

Risk: Energy measurement accuracy depends on Vehicle sensor integration. If sensors are poorly calibrated or noisy, NGHE Solution optimization will be based on unreliable data. Mitigation: Define sensor accuracy requirements and implement data validation in NGHE Solution.

Risk: Safety Services must provide safety constraints to NGHE Solution in real-time. If SafetyAlert interface is slow or unreliable, optimization may violate safety limits. Mitigation: Implement safety gates as hard constraints (not advisory) and test under failure scenarios.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify the boundary between Vehicle-level power control and NGHE Solution-level energy optimization. Vehicle should own: (1) real-time power measurement and reporting via Telemetry; (2) low-level control of propulsion and auxiliary systems. NGHE Solution should own: (1) energy efficiency calculation and trending; (2) optimization algorithms that recommend control adjustments; (3) enforcement of safety gates via SafetyAlert. Define explicit data contracts for Telemetry (measurement format, frequency, accuracy) and SafetyAlert (constraint format, update frequency) to ensure reliable integration.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-B (active energy optimization with real-time feedback) directly addresses the refined FR (enable operators to achieve energy_per_ton ≤ E_target). The DP provides the mechanisms (measurement, optimization, feedback) necessary to achieve the outcome. No functional gap detected. Coupling is acceptable and necessary.

diffusion_risks: Moderate risk: If Vehicle subsystem also implements energy optimization, algorithms may diverge. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership and interface contracts.
Low risk: Teleoperation feedback delivery introduces network latency. Mitigation: Specify latency requirements and implement local feedback loops.
Low risk: Safety Services must provide real-time constraints. Mitigation: Implement SafetyAlert as a hard constraint interface with defined update frequency.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner (containment_score 95). Energy management is a system-level concern that requires coordination across Vehicle and Teleoperation. Centralizing logic in NGHE Solution avoids diffusion and ensures consistent optimization. External interfaces (Telemetry, Tasking, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are well-defined and map to known interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible via controlled trials measuring energy_per_ton over a defined evaluation period. Telemetry interface provides real-time data for monitoring. Acceptance criteria (energy_per_ton ≤ E_target) are measurable and objective.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns energy management logic; Vehicle provides power measurement; Teleoperation delivers feedback; Safety Services enforces constraints. Ownership boundaries are well-defined and non-overlapping.

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-B to explicitly separate safety-critical control (hard constraints) from efficiency optimization (soft optimization within constraints). Define a safety gate that prevents optimization from reducing power reserves below a threshold.
Define latency requirements for Telemetry feedback (e.g., <500 ms for tele-operated mode) to ensure real-time effectiveness.

Establish data contracts for Telemetry (measurement format, frequency, accuracy) and SafetyAlert (constraint format, update frequency) to ensure reliable integration between NGHE Solution and Vehicle/Safety Services.
Clarify the boundary between Vehicle-level power control and NGHE Solution-level energy optimization to avoid diffusion and conflicting objectives.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-003 is not atomic and requires further decomposition. The refined FR identifies five sub-FRs (measurement, work output, feedback, optimization, reporting) that must be individually designed and allocated to implementation modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to establish clear ownership, interface contracts, and verification criteria for each sub-FR. The current level provides sufficient clarity on the top-level DP (active energy optimization) and IM (NGHE Solution) to support downward decomposition without revision.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) from all propulsion and auxiliary systems.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log tonnage moved (mass × distance or equivalent work metric).
CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall implement control optimization logic to minimize energy consumption while maintaining safety and productivity constraints.
CR-NGHE-003.5: The NGHE solution shall support energy efficiency reporting and analytics for Site Manager and Maintenance/Quality stakeholders.

assumptions: Energy consumption can be measured with sufficient accuracy and granularity (per subsystem or aggregate).
Optimization algorithms can be tuned to respect safety constraints without degrading efficiency gains.
Tele-operators have sufficient network latency and bandwidth to receive real-time feedback and adjust controls.
Operators will respond to efficiency feedback and modify behavior (behavioral adoption is external to system design).
Vehicle subsystem will provide power measurement data via Telemetry interface with defined accuracy and frequency.
Safety Services will provide safety constraints via SafetyAlert interface in real-time.
Energy efficiency target (E_target) is defined by customer and remains stable over evaluation period.
open_risks: Sensor noise or calibration drift could invalidate efficiency metrics. Mitigation: Define sensor accuracy requirements and implement data validation.
Conflicting objectives (efficiency vs. safety margin) may require trade-off analysis. Mitigation: Implement safety gates as hard constraints.
Network delays could degrade feedback effectiveness in tele-operated mode. Mitigation: Specify latency requirements and implement local feedback loops.
If Vehicle subsystem also implements energy optimization, algorithms may diverge. Mitigation: Establish clear ownership and interface contracts.
Behavioral adoption of efficiency feedback is uncertain. Mitigation: Implement training and incentive structures (external to system).
evidence_needed: Controlled trial data: energy_per_ton measurements over 40-hour evaluation period under representative worksite conditions.
Sensor accuracy specifications and calibration procedures for energy and work output measurement.
Network latency measurements for Telemetry feedback delivery in tele-operated mode.
Safety constraint definitions (min power reserve, no-optimization zones) from Safety Services.
Operator feedback on effectiveness of real-time efficiency feedback (usability study).
Comparison of energy efficiency with and without optimization logic enabled (A/B test).
